title: Eva Nutrition
wikitext:
[]: Eva Nutrition             Quote Post by Jobhug » Wed Feb 14, 2007 10:53 pm When the Mass production units jump Team Soryu we see that Nigoki has intestines. Is it ever explained as to whether the Evas have to eat? We see that they can eat and digest when Yui goes Berserk in the fight with Zeruel. The umbilical cable provides electricity to presumably send nerve signals up and down the massive bodies of the Evangelions, but most likely couldn't provide nutrition through the same tube. Possibilities: *The intestines are genetic throwbacks like the human appendix that serve no purpose except by chance, aiding in consumption and absorption of the S2 organ, which was never intended. *Eva units are fed somehow (intravienously or with a giant knife/fork/chopsticks or whatever) *Evas need to have intestines because the plot required Yui to eat Zeruel and Kyoko to be killed in a shocking and brutal manner.

[]: Re: Eva Nutrition             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Feb 14, 2007 11:13 pm Jobhug wrote: *The intestines are genetic throwbacks like the human appendix that serve no purpose except by chance, aiding in consumption and absorption of the S2 organ, which was never intended.

[]:             Quote Post by TriLink » Thu Feb 15, 2007 12:58 am The human intestines serve the purpose of breaking down food and allowing the nutrition from that food to be absorbed into the blood stream and disposing of the waste afterwards. In an Eva's case, they wouldn't be eating food because they would be getting their energy from another power source be it those electrical plugs, an S^2 Engine, etc. It would be enterting the blood via a different route and thus the intestines, although functional, would seldom be used. Or it could just be that guts helped Nigoki's death scene look WAAAAY COOOOLER. 8) Who knows.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Feb 15, 2007 1:18 am An Eva is a living biological entity. My assumption is that through cryostasis, the Evas are kept in a generally preserved state. The cryonic fluid probably also serves some nutrification purpses. The mechanical and biological systems of the Evas have to be maintained somehow. But things like intestines are probably fairly useless and redundant as the Evas are probably fed nutrients through intravenous methods.

[]:             Quote Post by Jobhug » Thu Feb 15, 2007 2:41 am The Eva Monkey wrote: . The cryonic fluid probably also serves some nutrification purpses.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Thu Feb 15, 2007 6:59 am Adam was asleep for 4billion years with no power supply of any kind. Logically they don't need to eat, but being humans have all the standard equipment that comes along with being such.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Feb 15, 2007 9:29 am Jobhug wrote: The Eva Monkey wrote: . The cryonic fluid probably also serves some nutrification purpses.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Thu Feb 15, 2007 9:59 am They are described as God-humans constructed from a different form of matter, that if left be will live forever, with wacky mass/shape shifting abilities provided they have the required equipment. I don't think normal rules apply to them. Adam and Lilith both did fine for billions of years without nourishment of any kind after all. Not aging implies their bodies don't break down in the same manor as ours do. Plus they weren't meant to eat in the first place, FoL and all.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Feb 15, 2007 10:04 am Anonymous_Evafan wrote: I don't think normal rules apply to them.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Thu Feb 15, 2007 12:00 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Then why put them in cryostasis?

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Thu Feb 15, 2007 2:17 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Then why put them in cryostasis?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Feb 15, 2007 3:01 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote: From ep #01 and ep #22 cryostasis seems to be a lockdown for misbehaving Evas.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Thu Feb 15, 2007 3:23 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Anonymous_Evafan wrote: From ep #01 and ep #22 cryostasis seems to be a lockdown for misbehaving Evas.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Feb 15, 2007 9:44 pm Why would a god have intestines? Easily, if you base a god on a mortal template -- "Man Makes God in His Own Image", blah blah blah. I'm looking at you, FAR. Regarding electricity... Scenario A: S2 organ generates energy --> ??? --> energy is provided to the body (etc.) Scenario B: Raw electricity fed into Evas --> ??? --> energy is provided to the body (etc.) ...wherein "???" is the means by which raw energy is "made usable" and disseminated. I've considered that, in Scenario B, Nerv is just taking advantage of "preexisting arrangements", if that makes any sense -- the energy is provided from a different source, but everything after that is the same.

[]:             Quote Post by ORAMI!? » Fri Feb 16, 2007 1:43 am I would guess that the Eva receive "nutrition" from the power supply (much like an Angel would receive "nutrition" from its FoL=S2), and that the cage liquid is used simply to help support the weight and pevent unnecessary deterioration when not operating. As for why they have intestines and all sorts of other seemingly needless/useless human organs, perhaps it is because they have to sync with human pilots who have those organs, so those neural connections are needed to increase the overal connection level and/or prevent "phantom limb" type reactions?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Feb 16, 2007 6:50 am ORAMI!? wrote: As for why they have intestines and all sorts of other seemingly needless/useless human organs

[]:             Quote Post by Jobhug » Fri Feb 16, 2007 8:03 am Reichu wrote: "It's okay, Shinji-kun, they're still the--" "MY TWIG AND TWO BERRIES?!?"

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Feb 16, 2007 2:24 pm Jobhug wrote: Honestly I don't think Shinji would have any problem synching with a female humanoid. *Recalls Shinjiquarium *

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Fri Feb 16, 2007 3:28 pm Reichu wrote: I don't think anyone has really come up with an explanation for that which takes the context into account.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Feb 16, 2007 5:56 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: You mean the context that Shinji has just been driven completly batshit loco?
